page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel processor. It features 4 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The memory configuration consists of 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The processor includes an L3 cache of 260 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 2 MB respectively. Advanced instruction sets are supported, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), alongside cryptographic acceleration flags such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance profiles. Single-core execution is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression all registering in the average tier. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. This includes weak multi-core scores in stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark CPU tests, alongside low memory bandwidth measurements for both small and large blocks. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks, also demonstrate lower-tier throughput. For AI workloads, the server achieves average text generation speeds on small 135M models, but prompt processing speeds for both small and medium models remain in the bottom quartile.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4-highmem-4 offers a high memory density per vCPU without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-throughput multi-core processing or massive memory bandwidth. The server is suited for workloads such as static web serving, single-threaded text and data processing, and lightweight caching layers where memory capacity is prioritized over raw computational speed.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel cores, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, including moderate single-threaded compression and text processing. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the lower quartile. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive applications that do not demand high parallel processing power. It is suited for static web serving, lightweight caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 32 GB RAM. It offers moderate single-core performance but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 Intel vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 server offering high memory density and dedicated Intel vCPUs for memory-bound, single-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM
Intel AVX-512 and AMX instruction set support
Average single-core and single-threaded processing performance
Lower-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth
Best suited for static web serving and lightweight caching
